speaker: VALENTINE
text: How use doth breed a habit in a man!

speaker: VALENTINE
text: This shadowy desert, unfrequented woods,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I better brook than flourishing peopled towns;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Here can I sit alone, unseen of any,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And to the nightingale’s complaining notes

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Tune my distresses and record my woes.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: O thou that dost inhabit in my breast,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Leave not the mansion so long tenantless

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Lest, growing ruinous, the building fall

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And leave no memory of what it was.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Repair me with thy presence, Sylvia;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Thou gentle nymph, cherish thy forlorn swain.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: What hallowing and what stir is this today?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: These are my mates, that make their wills their law,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Have some unhappy passenger in chase.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: They love me well, yet I have much to do

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To keep them from uncivil outrages.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Withdraw thee, Valentine. Who’s this comes here?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Madam, this service I have done for you—

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Though you respect not aught your servant doth—

speaker: PROTEUS
text: To hazard life, and rescue you from him

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That would have forced your honor and your love.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Vouchsafe me for my meed but one fair look;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: A smaller boon than this I cannot beg,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And less than this I am sure you cannot give.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: How like a dream is this I see and hear!

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Love, lend me patience to forbear awhile.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: O miserable, unhappy that I am!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Unhappy were you, madam, ere I came,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But by my coming, I have made you happy.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: By thy approach thou mak’st me most unhappy.

speaker: JULIA
text: And me, when he approacheth to your presence.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Had I been seizèd by a hungry lion,

speaker: SYLVIA
text: I would have been a breakfast to the beast

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Rather than have false Proteus rescue me.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: O heaven, be judge how I love Valentine,

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Whose life’s as tender to me as my soul;

speaker: SYLVIA
text: And full as much, for more there cannot be,

speaker: SYLVIA
text: I do detest false perjured Proteus.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Therefore begone; solicit me no more.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: What dangerous action, stood it next to death,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Would I not undergo for one calm look!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: O, ’tis the curse in love, and still approved,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: When women cannot love where they’re beloved.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: When Proteus cannot love where he’s beloved.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Read over Julia’s heart, thy first best love,

speaker: SYLVIA
text: For whose dear sake thou didst then rend thy faith

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Into a thousand oaths; and all those oaths

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Descended into perjury to love me.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Thou hast no faith left now unless thou ’dst two,

speaker: SYLVIA
text: And that’s far worse than none; better have none

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Than plural faith, which is too much by one.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: Thou counterfeit to thy true friend!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: In love

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Who respects friend?

speaker: SYLVIA
text: All men but Proteus.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Nay, if the gentle spirit of moving words

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Can no way change you to a milder form,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I’ll woo you like a soldier, at arms’ end,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And love you ’gainst the nature of love—force you.

speaker: SYLVIA
text: O, heaven!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I’ll force thee yield to my desire.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Ruffian, let go that rude uncivil touch,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Thou friend of an ill fashion.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Valentine!

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Thou common friend, that’s without faith or love,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: For such is a friend now. Treacherous man,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Thou hast beguiled my hopes; nought but mine eye

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Could have persuaded me. Now I dare not say

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I have one friend alive; thou wouldst disprove me.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Who should be trusted when one’s right hand

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is perjured to the bosom? Proteus,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I am sorry I must never trust thee more,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But count the world a stranger for thy sake.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: The private wound is deepest. O, time most accursed,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: ’Mongst all foes that a friend should be the worst!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: My shame and guilt confounds me.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Forgive me, Valentine. If hearty sorrow

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Be a sufficient ransom for offense,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I tender ’t here. I do as truly suffer

speaker: PROTEUS
text: As e’er I did commit.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Then I am paid,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And once again I do receive thee honest.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Who by repentance is not satisfied

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is nor of heaven nor Earth, for these are pleased;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: By penitence th’ Eternal’s wrath’s appeased.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And that my love may appear plain and free,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: All that was mine in Sylvia I give thee.

speaker: JULIA
text: O me unhappy!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Look to the boy.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Why, boy!

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Why, wag, how now? What’s the matter? Look up. Speak.

speaker: JULIA
text: O, good sir, my master charged

speaker: JULIA
text: me to deliver a ring to Madam Sylvia, which out of

speaker: JULIA
text: my neglect was never done.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Where is that ring, boy?

speaker: JULIA
text: Here ’tis; this is it.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: How, let me see.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Why, this is the ring I gave to Julia.

speaker: JULIA
text: O, cry you mercy, sir, I have mistook.

speaker: JULIA
text: This is the ring you sent to Sylvia.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But how cam’st thou by this ring? At my depart

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I gave this unto Julia.

speaker: JULIA
text: And Julia herself did give it me,

speaker: JULIA
text: And Julia herself hath brought it hither.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: How? Julia!

speaker: JULIA
text: Behold her that gave aim to all thy oaths

speaker: JULIA
text: And entertained ’em deeply in her heart.

speaker: JULIA
text: How oft hast thou with perjury cleft the root!

speaker: JULIA
text: O, Proteus, let this habit make thee blush.

speaker: JULIA
text: Be thou ashamed that I have took upon me

speaker: JULIA
text: Such an immodest raiment, if shame live

speaker: JULIA
text: In a disguise of love.

speaker: JULIA
text: It is the lesser blot, modesty finds,

speaker: JULIA
text: Women to change their shapes than men their minds.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: “Than men their minds”? ’Tis true. O heaven, were man

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But constant, he were perfect; that one error

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Fills him with faults, makes him run through all th’ sins;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Inconstancy falls off ere it begins.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: What is in Sylvia’s face but I may spy

speaker: PROTEUS
text: More fresh in Julia’s, with a constant eye?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Come, come, a hand from either.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Let me be blest to make this happy close.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: ’Twere pity two such friends should be long foes.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Bear witness, heaven, I have my wish forever.

speaker: JULIA
text: And I mine.

speaker: OUTLAWS
text: A prize, a prize, a prize!

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Forbear, forbear, I say. It is my lord the Duke.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Your Grace is welcome to a man disgraced,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Banished Valentine.

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir Valentine?

speaker: THURIO
text: Yonder is Sylvia, and Sylvia’s mine.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Thurio, give back, or else embrace thy death;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Come not within the measure of my wrath.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Do not name Sylvia thine; if once again,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Verona shall not hold thee. Here she stands;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Take but possession of her with a touch—

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I dare thee but to breathe upon my love!

speaker: THURIO
text: Sir Valentine, I care not for her, I.

speaker: THURIO
text: I hold him but a fool that will endanger

speaker: THURIO
text: His body for a girl that loves him not.

speaker: THURIO
text: I claim her not, and therefore she is thine.

speaker: DUKE
text: The more degenerate and base art thou

speaker: DUKE
text: To make such means for her as thou hast done,

speaker: DUKE
text: And leave her on such slight conditions.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Now, by the honor of my ancestry,

speaker: DUKE
text: I do applaud thy spirit, Valentine,

speaker: DUKE
text: And think thee worthy of an empress’ love.

speaker: DUKE
text: Know, then, I here forget all former griefs,

speaker: DUKE
text: Cancel all grudge, repeal thee home again,

speaker: DUKE
text: Plead a new state in thy unrivaled merit,

speaker: DUKE
text: To which I thus subscribe: Sir Valentine,

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou art a gentleman, and well derived;

speaker: DUKE
text: Take thou thy Sylvia, for thou hast deserved her.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I thank your Grace, the gift hath made me happy.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I now beseech you, for your daughter’s sake,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To grant one boon that I shall ask of you.

speaker: DUKE
text: I grant it for thine own, whate’er it be.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: These banished men, that I have kept withal,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Are men endued with worthy qualities.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Forgive them what they have committed here,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And let them be recalled from their exile;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: They are reformèd, civil, full of good,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And fit for great employment, worthy lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou hast prevailed; I pardon them and thee.

speaker: DUKE
text: Dispose of them as thou know’st their deserts.

speaker: DUKE
text: Come, let us go; we will include all jars

speaker: DUKE
text: With triumphs, mirth, and rare solemnity.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And as we walk along, I dare be bold

speaker: VALENTINE
text: With our discourse to make your Grace to smile.

speaker: VALENTINE
text:
Pointing
                 
                to
                 
                Julia
                .
                 
              What think you of this page, my lord?

speaker: DUKE
text: I think the boy hath grace in him; he blushes.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I warrant you, my lord, more grace than boy.

speaker: DUKE
text: What mean you by that saying?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Please you, I’ll tell you as we pass along,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: That you will wonder what hath fortunèd.—

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Come, Proteus, ’tis your penance but to hear

speaker: VALENTINE
text: The story of your loves discoverèd.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: That done, our day of marriage shall be yours,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: One feast, one house, one mutual happiness.
